skill.name: react-flow-best-practices
skill.description: Review and improve React Flow and @xyflow/react v12 canvases, including custom nodes, handles, edges, viewport behavior, accessibility, and performance.
skill.summary: Guides reviews and refactors for reliable React Flow workflow canvases.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium findings, but review found they are false positives from Markdown inline code, TypeScript examples, and React Flow terminology. No executable scripts, command execution paths, credential handling, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration patterns were found. A single hardcoded promotional URL remains a low-risk network concern.

low_findings.title: Promotional External Link In Guidance
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes a hardcoded external URL to Casely. This is not evidence of exfiltration or automatic network access, but it may steer users to an external commercial site.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly present in the guidance text and is the only confirmed network-related artifact. It is low risk because the skill does not fetch the URL or instruct automatic data transfer.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Crypto Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command execution and weak cryptography patterns appear in prose, YAML descriptions, Markdown inline code, and TypeScript examples. No runnable shell, Ruby, cryptographic API, or command invocation behavior was found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review confirms these locations contain documentation or static TypeScript examples. There is no evidence of executable command dispatch, weak crypto implementation, or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: React Flow apps can break when node IDs, handles, viewport behavior, or custom components drift from expected contracts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused reviews and practical refactors for @xyflow/react v12.
actual_capabilities: Reviews React Flow canvas structure, provider boundaries, and global CSS setup.
Checks node, edge, handle, sourceHandle, and targetHandle identity contracts.
Guides custom node, custom edge, NodeToolbar, and NodeResizer implementation reviews.
Identifies viewport behaviors that can override user pan or zoom state.
Highlights accessibility and keyboard concerns in workflow canvas interactions.
Provides a review checklist for risk-ranked React Flow findings.
limitations: It provides guidance and review structure, not automated code transformation.
It is focused on @xyflow/react v12 and may need current docs for later APIs.
It does not run the application or verify visual behavior by itself.
It may mention one external product link when context makes it relevant.

use_cases.title: Audit a Workflow Builder
use_cases.description: Review a canvas for broken handles, unstable node types, intrusive auto-fit behavior, and accessibility gaps before release.

use_cases.title: Refactor Custom Nodes
use_cases.description: Improve custom node typing, local interactions, drag safety, toolbar behavior, and resize persistence without changing product intent.

use_cases.title: Standardize Review Output
use_cases.description: Produce severity-ranked review notes with concrete risks, file references, and residual testing gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Canvas Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing React Flow canvas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the React Flow best practices skill to review this canvas. Focus on obvious correctness, accessibility, and viewport issues.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Node Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of custom nodes and handles.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these custom nodes, handles, and edges for stable IDs, typed data, drag safety, and interactive control behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Workflow Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning before changing a workflow builder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor plan for this React Flow workflow builder. Preserve saved flow compatibility and call out migration risks.

prompt_templates.title: Production Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit before shipping a complex canvas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a production readiness audit for this @xyflow/react v12 implementation. Rank findings by severity and include residual testing gaps.

output_examples.input: Review a canvas where edges sometimes disappear after nodes change.
output: High: Edge handles may not match rendered handle IDs after node updates.
Medium: Dynamic handle changes should trigger node internals recalculation.
Residual risk: Verify behavior by connecting, deleting, and restoring affected nodes.

output_examples.input: Check a workflow canvas that recenters on every status update.
output: Medium: Repeated auto-fit can override manual pan and zoom.
Recommendation: Treat viewport as user-owned after manual interaction.
Residual risk: Test project switch, first load, and explicit reset actions.

output_examples.input: Assess custom node controls inside draggable cards.
output: Medium: Interactive controls need drag and pan protection.
Low: Icon-only controls need accessible names.
Recommendation: Keep critical actions visible through selected or focused states.

best_practices: Map the canvas architecture before judging isolated React Flow details.
Keep node, edge, type, and handle IDs stable when flows may be persisted.
Report findings with concrete user impact and residual verification gaps.
anti_patterns: Do not recreate nodeTypes or edgeTypes inline during every render.
Do not let automatic fitView behavior fight manual pan or zoom.
Do not place interactive node controls without nodrag, nopan, or needed wheel protection.

faq.question: What React Flow version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets @xyflow/react v12 patterns and recommends checking current documentation when API behavior is uncertain.

faq.question: Can it fix my React Flow code automatically?
faq.answer: It guides reviews and refactors. Actual edits depend on the connected AI tool and project context.

faq.question: Does it cover custom nodes and edges?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for typed nodes, handles, toolbars, resizers, custom edges, and edge labels.

faq.question: Does it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks labels, keyboard behavior, focus states, controls, MiniMap, and color-only states.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No automatic network access is required. The skill text contains one optional external promotional link.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Frontend engineers, React developers, and reviewers working on workflow builders or node-based canvas interfaces should use it.
